offensive.name: Fracture Curse
offensive.description: Your spells fracture the very bones of your foes — degrading strength, compounding wounds, and eventually shattering named bones permanently.
tiers: On a failed save, the creature suffers a bone fracture — their Strength or Dexterity checks are reduced by 1d4 for 1 minute.
If the target already has a Wound, the fracture deals +2d6 additional damage and reduces their movement speed by 10 feet.
When you cause a creature to gain 2 or more Wounds from the same effect, they must succeed on a Constitution save or suffer an Injury — a broken limb, shattered ribs, or similar structural failure.

Once per long rest, name a specific bone (skull, spine, femur, etc.). If the target fails their save, that bone shatters — rendering the affected body part inoperable unless healed by Regenerate or high-level vitaemancy.

supportive.name: Marrowforge
supportive.description: You reinforce yourself with calcified will — building layers of bone over flesh until strikes shatter against you and enemies bleed for daring to try.
tiers: Gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus and +1 to AC for 1 round.
While active, gain resistance to bludgeoning damage, and your melee attacks deal +1d4 piercing or necrotic damage (your choice) as bone fragments extend from your strikes.
If you have at least 1 Wound, you gain immunity to critical hits and your Wound threshold increases by +1.

Enter Bone Bastion for 1 minute — you gain immunity to nonmagical weapons, enemies that strike you with melee weapons must succeed on a Dexterity save or take 2d8 slashing damage as bone shards erupt outward, and you may reattach or regenerate broken limbs as a bonus action.

supportive.name: Ossuary Invocation
supportive.description: You summon the memory of the dead through bone — speaking to what remains, raising skeletal champions, and eventually erupting a cathedral of the dead from the earth itself.
tiers: You may touch a bone and cast Speak with Dead without components — the memory held in the bone answers.
Revive a skeleton with limited autonomy for 10 minutes — useful for scouting, triggering traps, or operating mechanisms.
Call forth an ancestral skeleton of a slain warrior or beast — it appears for 1 minute as a CR 10 undead construct under your complete control.

Once per long rest, invoke a Bone Cathedral — within a 30-foot radius, bones erupt from the earth, all necrotic spells are empowered (+1 die), you may resurrect up to three skeletal champions (CR 10 each), and the cathedral persists for 1 hour, granting resistance to radiant damage and immunity to Turn Undead for all creatures you summon within it.

control.name: Ossify
control.description: You transform soft tissue into unyielding bone — immobilizing limbs, seizing joints, and eventually calcifying vital organs from the inside out.
tiers: One limb of your choice is partially ossified — the target has disadvantage on Dexterity checks involving that limb until the end of your next turn.
On a failed save, the limb becomes immobile for 1 round — natural or unarmed attacks made with it fail automatically.
You may now target two limbs or a jointed structure. Movement is reduced by 15 feet, and the creature suffers 1 Wound if they attempt to move with ossified parts.

You may ossify vital organs (heart, lungs, throat). On a failed save the creature gains 1 Injury, loses the ability to speak, breathe, or cast spells with verbal components for 1 minute, and takes 4d12 necrotic damage as their internals calcify.

control.name: Bone Puppeteer
control.description: You control skeletal structures with a snap of your will — animating the dead, restraining the living through their own bones, and puppeting the fallen as extensions of your intent.
tiers: Animate one nearby skeleton or bone pile as a minion for 1 minute. It follows simple mental commands (AC 13, HP 15, deals 1d6 damage).
You may control existing skeletons or target a living creature with visible bones. On a failed save they are Restrained as their own skeleton briefly resists them.
Use bone control to force a creature to move up to 15 feet against their will (as if grappled) or make a single melee attack against another creature of your choice.
Dominate up to three bone-possessing creatures simultaneously. Once per long rest, you may fully puppet an unconscious or paralyzed creature as if using Dominate Monster, bypassing immunities.

complementEffects.name: Splinter
complementEffects.description: The impact doesn't just wound — it fragments, sending bone shards tearing outward into every creature nearby.
complementEffects.effect: On a hit or failed saving throw against the primary effect, bone shards erupt from the point of impact. Each creature within 5 feet of the target (including the target) must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your Verum DC or take 1d8 piercing damage.
upgrades: The damage increases to 2d8, the radius increases to 10 feet, and creatures that fail have their movement speed reduced by 5 feet until they receive any healing.
The damage increases to 3d8, and creatures that fail the Dexterity save also have disadvantage on their next attack roll as embedded shards catch on muscle and joint.

complementEffects.name: Calcify
complementEffects.description: Structure hardens where structure is needed most — a thin layer of calcified bone forming over skin to absorb what follows.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect resolves, you or one ally within 30 feet gains temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your spellcasting modifier as a thin layer of calcified bone briefly forms over exposed skin.
upgrades: The temporary HP increases to twice your proficiency bonus + your spellcasting modifier, and the ally also gains +1 to AC until the temporary HP is depleted.
You may choose two creatures to receive this benefit, and while the temporary HP remains, each is immune to being knocked prone — the bone layer keeping them grounded and stable.

complementEffects.name: Joint Lock
complementEffects.description: The skeleton turns against its owner — joints seizing mid-motion, stripping speed and reaction before the target can understand what is happening.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect hits or a target fails their saving throw, that target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or have their movement speed reduced by 15 feet and lose their reaction until the end of their next turn.
upgrades: On a failed save, the target also cannot take the Dash action until the end of their next turn — their locked joints making any burst of speed impossible.
Creatures that fail are also unable to make opportunity attacks and suffer disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws until the end of their next turn — every joint in their body working against them.
